message:
feat(atc): effect semantics model, family-based messaging, single-entry eviction, regime discount reset

Introduces structured effect semantics that make ATC interventions
self-documenting and adds several correctness fixes.

Effect semantics model (atc.rs):

- New AtcEffectSemantics struct captures per-effect-family metadata:
  risk_level, utility_model, operator_action (what the recipient
  should do), remediation (how to resolve), escalation_policy (what
  happens next if unresolved), evidence_summary, cooldown_key,
  cooldown_micros, requires_project, ack_required,
  high_risk_intervention, and preconditions.

- Added `semantics` field to AtcEffectPlan so every generated effect
  carries its own semantic contract.

- New effect_semantics_for() method returns family-specific semantics
  for: liveness_monitoring, deadlock_remediation, liveness_probe,
  reservation_release, release_notice, and withheld_release_notice.
  Each family defines calibrated risk levels, operator-facing
  remediation instructions, escalation policies, and cooldown windows.

- build_effect() now takes a `family` parameter and includes it in
  the effect seed for deterministic effect_id generation.

Family-based messaging (lib.rs):

- Effect cooldown deduplication now uses semantics.cooldown_key
  instead of ad-hoc kind:category:project:agent concatenation.

- Effect subject lines rewritten per-family with operator-friendly
  descriptions (e.g. "activity check" instead of "send_advisory",
  "acknowledgment requested" instead of "probe_agent").

- Effect headlines rewritten per-family with actionable context
  explaining what ATC observed and what it expects.

- All effects now always generate a message body (never None),
  ensuring every intervention has an operator-visible audit trail.

Single-entry eviction (atc_open_index.rs):

- evict_oldest() now removes ONE entry at the oldest timestamp
  instead of all entries at that timestamp. Under high load, many
  experiences share a creation timestamp (same ATC tick) — batch
  eviction was too aggressive and could drop the entire tick's worth
  of open experiences when the index was at capacity.

Regime discount reset (atc_regime.rs):

- RegimeManager now resets discount_applied=false on confirmed regime
  transitions. Previously, the flag stayed true after the first
  transition, preventing history discounting from being re-applied
  on subsequent regime changes.

Also updates beads issue tracker metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-0qt6e.2.4","title":"ATC Learning 2.4: wire reservation, conflict, and deadlock events into outcome resolution","description":"# ATC Learning 2.4: Wire reservation, conflict, and deadlock events into outcome resolution

## Why
Release requests, conflict advisories, and deadlock interventions need to be judged by what actually happened to reservations and conflict structure afterward.

## Deliverables
- wire reservation grant, release, and conflict signals into the resolver
- connect deadlock-cycle changes and blocked-edge disappearance to relevant experiences
- define how reservation-side events resolve release, conflict, and intervention actions
- preserve project scoping so cross-project noise does not contaminate labels
- ensure graph maintenance and outcome resolution agree on the same underlying event semantics

## Acceptance Criteria
- reservation and deadlock outcomes are derived from authoritative live signals rather than stale inferred state or test scaffolding
- project scoping and deduplication rules are explicit enough to prevent false labels from repeated or cross-project events
- conflict-graph maintenance and outcome resolution consume one coherent event model instead of diverging over time

## Important Notes
This is the bridge between the conflict graph and the learning ledger.  {"id":"br-0qt6e.2.5","title":"ATC Learning 2.5: open-experience index and timed/censored resolution engine","description":"# ATC Learning 2.5: Open-experience index and timed/censored resolution engine

## Why
Delayed feedback is normal in ATC. We need a small, explicit unresolved set rather than scanning history to see what is still waiting on evidence.

## Deliverables
- maintain an in-memory open-experience index keyed by decision or experience identity
- maintain a bounded per-target or per-cohort frontier of open candidate causes so overlapping interventions can be inspected for eligibility without rescanning history
- drive timed resolution and expiration windows from that index
- emit explicit censored, expired, or causally-confounded outcomes when evidence never arrives cleanly enough to support a trustworthy label
- ensure restart and recovery semantics for open rows are well-defined
- keep memory bounded and lookup O(1)

## Acceptance Criteria
- the open set stays bounded, restart-recoverable, and explicit enough to support long-delay, overlap-heavy, and crash-recovery scenarios without guesswork
- expiration, censoring, and confounded-attribution states become first-class states visible to rollups and operator summaries rather than silent row mutations
- the plan guarantees that the system can answer both \"what is still unresolved?\" and \"what is unresolved because competing causes overlap?\" without any raw-history scan

## Important Notes
This bead is the runtime backbone of delayed attribution. Without it, the rest of the math is mostly ornamental or causally overconfident.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:54:46.913114272Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T09:07:44.430114826Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T09:07:44.429567520Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met. Tracks resolved/expired/censored/evicted counters. Detects causal confounding (overlapping interventions for same agent).  {"id":"br-0qt6e.2.6","title":"ATC Learning 2.6: make live execution the default and replace fake probe intent with a real probe action","description":"# ATC Learning 2.6: Make live execution the default and replace fake probe intent with a real probe action

## Why
ATC cannot learn from real outcomes if the normal mode is inert or if probe actions are placeholder strings instead of real effects.

## Deliverables
- make live execution the default executor mode while preserving explicit override knobs
- replace the probe-intent placeholder with a real probe action path
- ensure probe executions are durable, attributable, and visible to the operator surfaces
- record intervention telemetry whenever the operator loop actually executes an effect
- remove or demote public-facing shadow semantics so they no longer define the system's default behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- the default operator posture is live execution, with any non-live mode remaining an explicit override rather than the ambient behavior
- fake probe intent is removed from the normal path and replaced by a real, durable, attributable probe effect
- executed, suppressed, or skipped probe behavior is visible to learning state, operator surfaces, and verification scenarios instead of disappearing behind placeholder semantics

## Important Notes
This bead is both a utility fix and a prerequisite for meaningful learning.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonSnow","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:54:55.839174696Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T09:08:38.256778235Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T09:08:38.256248102Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met. -{"id":"br-0qt6e.2.7","title":"ATC Learning 2.7: audit and tighten release/advisory/probe effect semantics for safety and utility","description":"# ATC Learning 2.7: Audit and tighten release/advisory/probe effect semantics for safety and utility

## Why
The earlier ATC completion work explicitly called for reviewing whether advisory, release, and probe effects are safe, useful, and worth the operator or user disruption they create. That concern deserves a first-class implementation bead.

## Deliverables
- audit the current effect families for unsafe, noisy, or low-value behavior
- tighten message content, execution rules, and preconditions for release, advisory, and probe actions
- distinguish clearly between low-risk nudges and high-risk interventions
- define repetition-suppression, cooldown, escalation, and operator-facing remediation rules so repeated low-value advisories or probes do not become spam
- ensure outward-facing effect content stays concise, actionable, and tied to the concrete signal, evidence, and next-step guidance that triggered it
- make the tightened semantics visible to the outcome resolver and learning system
- document the intended utility model for each effect family so later adaptation is optimizing something real

## Acceptance Criteria
- each effect family has explicit utility, safety, and precondition rules that later adaptation and user-surface beads can rely on without reinterpretation
- low-risk nudges and high-risk interventions are separated clearly enough that exploration and admissibility policies do not blur them later
- repeated low-value or redundant effects are explicitly suppressed, cooled down, or escalated according to deterministic rules rather than left to ad hoc caller behavior
- user/operator-facing effect content gives concrete remediation and evidence pointers instead of generic noise
- any effect family that remains too noisy or unsafe to justify optimization is explicitly constrained rather than silently kept in scope

## Important Notes
This bead is about making the actions themselves worth learning over. A bad action family with a perfect learner is still a bad system.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:19:59.609918528Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-17T21:48:21.816520132Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","atc","atc-learning","atc-learning-wiring"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.2.7","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:37:34.427249307Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.2.7","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:19:59.609918528Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.2.7","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:22:47.622733169Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.2.7","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:22:56.552527270Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.2.7","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:23:06.099616857Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.2.7","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:23:14.985401594Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-0qt6e.2.8","title":"ATC Learning 2.8: thread participation graph and session-synthesis wiring from live message events","description":"# ATC Learning 2.8: Thread participation graph and session-synthesis wiring from live message events

## Why
The earlier ATC completion backlog explicitly called out wiring real domain message events into session summaries and thread participation tracking. That signal is also important for learning features and for understanding coordination pressure before conflicts erupt.

## Deliverables
- wire live message events into the thread participation graph, not just into raw message counters
- make session synthesis and thread-level coordination features update from the same production event stream
- expose the derived participation state to the learning feature pipeline without rescanning history
- keep participation updates deterministic and project-scoped
- document how thread participation influences advisories, probes, and downstream risk signals

## Acceptance Criteria
- participation and session-synthesis state update from the production event stream only, not from periodic rescans or test-only reconstruction
- the derived state is deterministic and project-scoped enough that summaries, learning features, and tests can all consume it without disagreement
- the bead makes clear how thread participation changes downstream risk and actionability instead of leaving it as an ornamental metric

## Important Notes
This bead ensures message traffic affects more than inbox counts. It becomes a real coordination signal in the ATC kernel.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:20:08.969216491Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T09:12:20.037723512Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T09:12:20.035089067Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met: atc_participation.rs provides ParticipationSnapshot, ParticipationFeatures (quantized for learning pipeline), ParticipationEvent types for the production event stream, ParticipationRiskSignals, and PARTICIPATION_FEATURE_MAPPING documenting how thread participation influences conflict risk priors, advisory targeting, and probe priority. 10 passing tests, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","atc","atc-learning","atc-learning-wiring"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.2.8","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.1.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:37:48.613238650Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.2.8","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:20:08.969216491Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.2.8","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:23:43.804517216Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-0qt6e.2.9","title":"ATC Learning 2.9: conflict-graph maintenance and blocking-conflict ingestion from live reservation results","description":"# ATC Learning 2.9: Conflict-graph maintenance and blocking-conflict ingestion from live reservation results

## Why
The earlier ATC completion work explicitly called out two separate needs: keep the conflict graph updated from live grant or release events, and ingest blocking-conflict information from reservation conflict tool results. That wiring needs to be explicit, not assumed.

## Deliverables
- parse live reservation conflict results into blocking-conflict observations usable by ATC
- update conflict-graph state from reservation grant and release events in production paths
- ensure conflict-graph maintenance and learning or outcome attribution share the same event semantics
- prevent duplicate or stale conflict edges from distorting later labels and summaries
- document the authoritative source of truth for blocking relationships versus active reservations

## Acceptance Criteria
- live reservation results produce authoritative blocking-conflict observations with explicit deduplication and stale-edge handling
- conflict-graph state, outcome resolution, and operator summaries all consume the same conflict semantics instead of drifting apart
- the bead leaves no ambiguity about whether production runtime events or test scaffolding own the conflict graph

## Important Notes
This bead exists because the conflict graph is only useful if it is fed by real runtime events rather than test scaffolding or stale inferred state.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:20:18.199605118Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T08:43:00.491803437Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T08:43:00.491278534Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met. (2) derive_release_domain_events calls atc::atc_note_reservation_released(). (3) derive_force_release_domain_events calls atc::atc_note_reservation_released().  {"id":"br-0qt6e.3","title":"ATC Learning Phase 3: adaptive math stack and safety envelope","description":"# Phase 3: Adaptive Math Stack and Safety Envelope

This phase turns captured experiences into useful adaptation. It covers outcome labeling, sufficient statistics, shrinkage, conformal budgets, regret-bounded policy updates, regime detection, fairness/anti-starvation constraints, contamination/anti-gaming robustness, interference-aware credit assignment, controller composition, and automatic rollback.

## Deliverables
- deterministic outcome automata
- materialized sufficient-stat rollups
- shrinkage across sparse cohorts
- conformal risk budgets by action family and stratum
- finite-policy adaptation with exploration constraints
- fairness, anti-starvation, and per-entity harm budgets
- contamination detection, influence-limiting, and anti-gaming safeguards for strategic environments
- interference-aware attribution, causal eligibility windows, and confounded-outcome handling for overlapping interventions or exogenous changes
- regime detection, control composition, and safe rollback logic

## Acceptance Criteria
- the phase yields bounded adaptation over an explicit policy family rather than unconstrained self-modification
- sparse data, drift, stressed risk budgets, fairness/harm-budget pressure, suspicious or contaminated evidence, and causally confounded outcomes all lead to conservative behavior that is inspectable and testable
- later user-surface and verification beads can explain both action and deliberate non-action, including fairness-driven vetoes, contamination-driven distrust, or robustness-driven throttles, from the contracts defined here
- the phase makes it explicit when ATC may learn from an outcome, when it must defer or censor because attribution is ambiguous, and how that choice becomes visible to users and audits","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:51:15.064394016Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T01:50:16.457859426Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","atc","atc-learning","atc-learning-phase3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:51:15.064394016Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1244,"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3","author":"codex","text":"Phase 3 is intentionally conservative.  {"id":"br-0qt6e.3.1","title":"ATC Learning 3.1: outcome labeling automata and censoring rules by action family","description":"# ATC Learning 3.1: Outcome labeling automata and censoring rules by action family

## Why
ATC needs deterministic, machine-checkable rules for what counts as success, failure, suppression, or censoring. Without that, the learning loop will drift into ad-hoc label noise.

## Deliverables
- define action-family-specific labeling rules for probe, advisory, release, conflict, and no-op/suspect actions
- define how safety-gated suppression, throttling, and skipped execution states feed into the labeling automata
- define attribution windows, causal eligibility windows, and delay handling
- define how overlapping eligible causes, exogenous recoveries, or concurrent operator changes downgrade confidence, split credit in bounded ways, or force censoring instead of false precision
- define censoring reasons, ambiguity/confoundedness reasons, and expiration behavior
- define realized_loss and reward semantics consistently across action families
- document ambiguous cases and how they are downgraded to censoring or explicit confounded-attribution states instead of forced labels

## Acceptance Criteria
- the labeling automata consume real execution-truth states rather than inferring outcomes from intent alone
- acted, deliberately non-acted, safety-suppressed, and causally confounded paths all have explicit deterministic treatment
- the plan explicitly prevents overlapping interventions or natural recovery from being silently converted into confident single-cause labels
- future verification beads can turn this bead directly into replay expectations and property tests

## Important Notes
This bead turns execution truth plus delayed evidence into stable labels. If it is vague, the rest of the adaptation stack becomes statistically noisy or causally dishonest.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:56:34.972689567Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T08:59:02.567637083Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T08:59:02.567116297Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met: atc_labeling.rs provides deterministic outcome labeling automata with 7 action-family-specific rules (probe, advisory, release, force_reservation, routing, backpressure, no_action), 8 censoring reasons, attribution windows per effect kind, non-execution path handling, and label_to_outcome conversion. 20 passing tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","atc","atc-learning","atc-learning-math"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.1","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:03:24.195808295Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.1","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:03:33.189131065Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.1","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:14:20.648074450Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.1","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:56:34.972689567Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-0qt6e.3.10","title":"ATC Learning 3.9: safe policy-improvement certificates with doubly-robust off-policy evaluation and anytime-valid confidence sequences","description":"# ATC Learning 3.9: Safe policy-improvement certificates with doubly-robust off-policy evaluation and anytime-valid confidence sequences

## Why
Policy promotion is the single highest-leverage and highest-blast-radius adaptive move in the ATC design. A candidate policy can look better in simple regret tables while still being biased by selection effects, weak support overlap, optional stopping, or hidden tail-risk on user-facing noise. The plan needs an explicit mathematical certification layer that blocks promotion unless ATC can demonstrate, with auditable evidence, that the candidate is better enough and safe enough to replace the incumbent.

## Deliverables
- define the promotion-certificate artifact that must exist before any candidate policy revision can displace the incumbent
- define the regime-aware off-policy evaluation stack for candidate-versus-incumbent comparison using doubly-robust or augmented inverse-propensity estimation rather than raw win-rate heuristics
- define overlap/support diagnostics, clipped or stabilized importance-weight controls, and refusal-to-certify behavior when counterfactual support is too weak
- define anytime-valid confidence-sequence or e-process style evidence thresholds so policy promotion remains valid under continuous monitoring and optional stopping
- define tail-risk and operator-noise protection so a candidate cannot be promoted on mean utility alone while worsening false-action spikes, spamminess, or other bad tail outcomes
- define blocker reason codes and machine-readable certificate fields for sparse data, unstable regime, overlap failure, weak lower bound, tail-budget violation, or unresolved attribution backlog
- make certificates traceable to policy IDs, revisions, regimes, strata, experience slices, and comparator baselines so later audits and surfaces can explain both promotions and non-promotions
- define what evidence can still be reused across regime boundaries and what must be reset, discounted, or explicitly marked as non-transferable

## Acceptance Criteria
- no candidate policy can be promoted without a positive, auditable improvement certificate against the incumbent rather than a heuristic score alone
- the design uses refusal-to-certify as a first-class safe outcome when overlap, attribution quality, or regime stability is inadequate
- promotion requires non-inferiority or bounded degradation on user-noise and tail-risk budgets, not just average reward improvement
- downstream summary, audit, explainability, unit-test, E2E, and rollout beads can all point to this bead as the canonical contract for why a promotion was allowed or denied

## Important Notes
This bead is the bridge between causal inference, sequential validity, tail-risk control, and operator trust. It is deliberately stronger than a generic "candidate scoring" story: the whole point is to turn policy promotion into something that is certifiable, inspectable, and safely blockable.","notes":"Duplicate of canonical safe policy-improvement certificate bead br-0qt6e.3.9","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T22:09:58.023025431Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T01:54:01.791422386Z","closed_at":"2026-03-17T22:17:54.991575221Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"br-0qt6e.3.11","title":"ATC Learning 3.11: fairness, anti-starvation, and per-agent/project harm budgets","description":"# ATC Learning 3.11: Fairness, anti-starvation, and per-agent/project harm budgets

## Why
A globally efficient ATC can still be user-hostile if it repeatedly burdens the same agent, project, or cohort because those cases are easier to measure, easier to optimize against, or more visible in the telemetry. The learning stack needs an explicit fairness and anti-starvation contract so it does not concentrate probes, advisories, suppressions, false positives, or intervention friction onto the same targets while claiming aggregate utility gains.

## Deliverables
- define the fairness observables ATC should track across agents, projects, and other relevant cohorts: intervention frequency, false-action exposure, suppression exposure, unresolved-backlog burden, probe burden, and operator-noise concentration
- define anti-starvation rules so quiet, low-volume, or low-visibility agents/projects are not systematically ignored when they actually need attention
- define per-agent, per-project, or per-cohort harm budgets and concentration limits that constrain both act-time decisions and policy adaptation
- define the interaction between global expected utility and fairness constraints, including when a fairness or anti-starvation guard vetoes an otherwise attractive action or policy move
- define machine-readable fairness debt, burden-concentration, and most-impacted-target indicators that can flow into summaries, audits, and rollout checks
- define how fairness state decays, resets, or is reweighted across regime changes so old concentration does not either vanish too fast or poison the future forever
- document what forms of asymmetry are acceptable, what forms are suspicious, and what evidence would justify deliberate unequal treatment

## Acceptance Criteria
- no agent, project, or tracked cohort can absorb an unbounded or poorly explained share of probes, advisories, suppressions, or false-action harm simply because it is easier for the learner to optimize there
- the fairness contract is explicit enough that later surfaces, audits, and tests can distinguish healthy prioritization from harmful concentration or starvation
- the design states what happens when fairness constraints conflict with short-horizon global utility, including which side wins and how that decision is made visible
- future verification beads can derive anti-starvation, bounded-concentration, and fairness-budget tests from this bead alone without chat archaeology

## Important Notes
This bead is not about flattening all distinctions or banning prioritization. It is about preventing a mathematically sophisticated system from becoming quietly unfair, annoying, or trust-eroding to the exact users it is supposed to help.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:07:29.647433405Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T01:46:00.522782497Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","atc","atc-learning","atc-learning-math"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.11","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:07:40.886411615Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.11","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:07:51.908620261Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.11","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:07:29.647433405Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.11","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:46:00.521410217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.11","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:08:03.101184105Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.11","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:08:14.820272060Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.11","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:09:00.796091498Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.11","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:09:33.797515848Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.11","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:09:44.535174Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-0qt6e.3.12","title":"ATC Learning 3.12: feedback-loop contamination detection, anti-gaming safeguards, and adversarial robustness","description":"# ATC Learning 3.12: Feedback-loop contamination detection, anti-gaming safeguards, and adversarial robustness

## Why
ATC is not learning from a neutral laboratory. It learns from agents, operators, and systems that can respond strategically or accidentally distort the evidence stream. Interventions can change observability, agents can learn to look alive without being healthy, noisy cohorts can dominate labels, and duplicated/delayed/replayed signals can make the learner over-trust bad evidence. Without an explicit anti-gaming and contamination contract, the rest of the math can still be elegant while the system learns the wrong lesson.

## Deliverables
- define the contamination and strategic-behavior threat model for ATC learning: replayed or duplicated events, spoofed liveness, performative acknowledgements, message floods, reservation churn, operator-induced selective labeling, coordinated agent behavior, intervention-caused observability shifts, and other self-fulfilling or self-canceling feedback loops
- define the observables and machine-readable suspicion states for evidence quality at the experience, actor, cohort, policy, and regime levels
- define how suspicious evidence is downweighted, quarantined, capped in influence, excluded from promotion evidence, or retained only for monitoring rather than control
- define robust aggregation or influence-limiting rules so a small number of adversarial or low-quality traces cannot dominate policy updates, fairness state, or previews
- define how contamination suspicion affects admissibility gates, certificates, previews, summaries, fairness logic, rollback triggers, and operator-visible reason codes
- define how ATC distinguishes real improvement from intervention-shaped observability artifacts, label leakage, or strategic adaptation by the agents it supervises
- define recovery, decay, and re-entry rules so contaminated evidence does not poison the future forever but also cannot be forgotten instantly without explanation
- define how audit artifacts and operator surfaces expose "data not trusted" conditions without collapsing into paranoia or noisy false alarms
- define efficiency constraints so robustness checks use maintained statistics, bounded state, and targeted diagnostics instead of expensive full-history rescans

## Acceptance Criteria
- the plan explicitly prevents policy promotion, preview trust, or unsafe action confidence from silently relying on obviously suspicious or strategically contaminated evidence
- future surfaces and tests can explain when ATC is acting on trusted evidence, when it is monitoring but not trusting certain data, and why that happened
- the robustness contract is specific enough that unit tests, E2E scenarios, audits, and rollout gates can derive anti-gaming and contamination cases from this bead alone without chat archaeology
- the bead strengthens the learning system without turning it into opaque paranoia theater or unbounded forensic overhead

## Important Notes
This bead is not optional hardening polish. It is a core requirement for any autonomous learning loop that operates in a strategic environment and supervises actors that can react to the system itself.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:54:50.696976001Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T09:40:30.312811097Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T09:40:30.312161992Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met. atc_contamination.rs: EvidenceQuality enum (Trusted/Suspect/Quarantined/Capped) with weight multipliers. 9 ContaminationKind types (DuplicateTrace, EventBurst, SpoofedLiveness, PerformativeAck, ReservationChurn, InfluenceDomination, ObservabilityArtifact, OperatorIntervention, CoordinatedBehavior).  {"id":"br-0qt6e.3.13","title":"ATC Learning 3.13: interference-aware credit assignment, overlapping intervention attribution, and causal eligibility windows","description":"# ATC Learning 3.13: Interference-aware credit assignment, overlapping intervention attribution, and causal eligibility windows

## Why
ATC does not operate in a clean single-cause world. Multiple interventions can overlap in time, operators can change controls mid-stream, exogenous project events can resolve a problem naturally, and one action can change the observability of another. If the learner credits or blames the wrong cause, it can look mathematically disciplined while adapting in the wrong direction. This bead exists to make attribution itself first-class, rather than assuming every resolved outcome belongs cleanly to one decision.

## Deliverables
- define the interference model for ATC learning: simultaneous or overlapping interventions, operator control changes, natural recoveries, exogenous load or coordination shifts, and observation-only events that occur inside an attribution window
- define causal eligibility windows and candidate-cause sets so the system knows which open experiences are even eligible to receive credit or blame for a given downstream outcome
- define how overlapping eligible causes are handled: downgraded confidence, censoring, explicit ambiguity states, bounded multi-touch credit, or "do not learn from this" outcomes when identifiability is too weak
- define how attribution confidence or confoundedness is represented in experience rows, rollups, summaries, previews, certificates, and audit artifacts
- define how interference-aware attribution interacts with contamination detection, fairness/burden accounting, regime changes, and rollout decisions so overlapping causes do not silently distort other guardrails
- define efficient runtime structures for maintaining the open-experience frontier per target or cohort, candidate-cause lookup, and attribution resolution without full-history rescans or unbounded combinatorial blowups
- define how the system distinguishes true improvements from natural recovery, parallel operator intervention, or competing ATC actions that would otherwise produce false learning signals
- define operator-visible reason codes and explainability hooks for "improved but attribution uncertain", "confounded by concurrent change", and "insufficient causal isolation to update policy"

## Acceptance Criteria
- the plan explicitly prevents ATC from treating every resolved outcome as a clean label when overlapping causes or exogenous changes make attribution ambiguous
- later math, surface, and verification beads can explain when ATC learned from an outcome, when it refused to learn, and why attribution confidence was downgraded
- the attribution contract is strong enough that summaries, previews, audit artifacts, unit tests, E2E scripts, and rollout reviews can all reuse it without inventing incompatible causal stories
- the design keeps attribution honest without exploding runtime cost, history scans, or operator cognitive overhead

## Important Notes
This bead is the causal honesty layer for the learning loop. It should make the system more trustworthy to users by teaching it to say "multiple things happened; I cannot honestly assign credit yet" instead of silently learning the wrong lesson.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:44:27.884027330Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T09:29:57.638511803Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T09:29:57.638019131Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met. atc_attribution.rs: AttributionConfidence enum (Clean/Primary/Shared/Ambiguous/Censored) with learning_weight (1.0/0.8/0.5/0/0). attribute_outcome() algorithm: 1) filter by eligibility window, 2) single=Clean, 3) disjoint subsystems=Clean, 4) dominant recency (2x)=Primary, 5) close overlap=Shared.  {"id":"br-0qt6e.3.14","title":"ATC Learning 3.14: safe value-of-information control, identifiability debt, and targeted low-risk experiment design","description":"# ATC Learning 3.14: Safe value-of-information control, identifiability debt, and targeted low-risk experiment design

## Why
Even with strong logging, certificates, contamination defenses, and interference-aware attribution, ATC can still get stuck in uncertainty-limited regimes where the system cannot safely learn enough from ambient production behavior alone. The smartest next step is to make ATC an active but disciplined scientist: it should know when uncertainty, confounding, or sparse support is blocking improvement, maintain an explicit identifiability-debt ledger, and spend tiny, low-risk, budgeted experiments or probes only when the expected information value clearly beats the user-noise and safety cost.

## Deliverables
- define an identifiability-debt ledger for action families, cohorts, and policy questions where current evidence is insufficient for confident adaptation, certification, or attribution
- define a safe value-of-information or knowledge-gradient scoring layer that estimates the expected learning value of a candidate probe, advisory variant, randomized tie-break, or low-risk intervention against its user-noise, fairness, contamination, and tail-risk cost
- define experiment eligibility gates so only low-risk, reversibly bounded actions can ever be chosen for information gain, and only when the normal policy is uncertainty-limited rather than already decision-sufficient
- define experiment budget tables across action family, cohort, regime, and time window so learning pressure cannot silently grow into spam, unfair burden concentration, or operational chaos
- define how targeted experiments are announced, previewed, logged, and linked to the experience ledger so later attribution, certificates, and audits know that an action had both control value and information value
- define how active experiments interact with contamination defense, interference-aware attribution, fairness budgets, and rollback so the system never learns aggressively from suspicious or strategically distorted settings
- define when the correct decision is "do not experiment" because the regime is unstable, the evidence is contaminated, the likely user burden is too high, or the incremental information value is too small
- define compact runtime artifacts for the whole stack: identifiability-debt tables, value-of-information scores, experiment-eligibility automata, budgeted experiment scheduler, and machine-readable reason codes for why an information-seeking action was allowed or refused

## Acceptance Criteria
- the plan explicitly upgrades ATC from passive learning only to safe active information acquisition, but only inside a strict low-risk and low-noise envelope
- later surfaces and tests can explain when a probe or intervention was issued partly to reduce uncertainty, what debt it was meant to repay, and why that was worth the cost
- the design makes "do not experiment" a first-class safe outcome when information value, fairness, contamination, or operator burden do not justify action
- the new learning mechanism is specific enough that summaries, previews, explainability cards, E2E scripts, and rollout gates can all reuse it without inventing incompatible stories

## Important Notes
This bead is the dual-control / active-experimentation layer for ATC. It is radically accretive because it lets the system learn faster and more honestly under sparse or confounded evidence, while still staying bounded, auditable, and user-respectful.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:56:28.532130279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T01:56:28.532130279Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","atc","atc-learning","atc-learning-math"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.14","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:56:28.532130279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.14","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:56:28.532130279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.14","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:56:28.532130279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.14","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:56:28.532130279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.14","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:56:28.532130279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.14","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:56:28.532130279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.14","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:56:28.532130279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.14","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:56:28.532130279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.14","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:56:28.532130279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.14","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-0  {"id":"br-0qt6e.3.6","title":"ATC Learning 3.6: regime detection, hysteresis, and history discounting","description":"# ATC Learning 3.6: Regime detection, hysteresis, and history discounting

## Why
History cannot be pooled forever as if workloads were stationary. ATC needs to know when the environment has changed enough that old evidence should count less.

## Deliverables
- define the changepoint or drift signal ATC will monitor
- define regime IDs, hysteresis, minimum dwell or stabilization rules, and transition criteria
- define what gets reset, discounted, frozen, or merely annotated across regime changes
- gate policy promotion during unstable transitions
- define operator-visible transition reasons and compact “what changed” summaries so a regime flip is diagnosable rather than mysterious
- make regime state inspectable from summaries and operator tooling

## Acceptance Criteria
- regime transitions are explicit, inspectable, and protected by hysteresis so the system does not flap between states under noisy evidence
- old evidence cannot silently contaminate new policy movement after a real regime shift
- transition states are explainable enough that downstream surfaces and audits can show whether the system is stable, transitioning, cooling, or frozen
- later audit, surface, and verification beads can tell when the system is stable, transitioning, or frozen from this bead alone

## Important Notes
This bead is the nonstationarity contract.  {"id":"br-0qt6e.3.7","title":"ATC Learning 3.7: admissibility gates, exploration budget, and automatic rollback","description":"# ATC Learning 3.7: Admissibility gates, exploration budget, and automatic rollback

## Why
Adaptive control without a strict safety envelope is not acceptable here. The system needs explicit bounds on when it may act, when it may explore, when suspicious evidence should reduce trust instead of increasing confidence, and when it must retreat.

## Deliverables
- define the action-tier model separating low-risk and high-risk actions
- define the exploration budget and the conditions under which exploration is allowed
- define the admissibility gate that combines calibration, conformal budgets, regime state, sparse-data checks, and contamination-suspicion or evidence-quality checks
- define automatic rollback triggers and fallback-to-incumbent behavior
- document why high-risk actions remain conservative even when low-risk actions adapt, and why suspicious evidence should tighten rather than relax the gate

## Acceptance Criteria
- exploration is permitted only for explicitly allowed low-risk action families under a bounded budget with auditable triggers
- rollback and fallback-to-incumbent rules are machine-readable and operator-visible rather than hidden in prose or implementation folklore
- the safety envelope makes suppressed or blocked actions legible instead of collapsing them into ambiguous non-behavior
- the gate can explicitly refuse action or refuse to trust recent evidence when contamination, gaming, or observability distortion is suspected

## Important Notes

 {"id":"br-0qt6e.3.8","title":"ATC Learning 3.8: controller-composition rules, timescale separation, and anti-oscillation design","description":"# ATC Learning 3.8: Controller-composition rules, timescale separation, and anti-oscillation design

## Why
The learning stack combines multiple adaptive controllers: calibration/e-process alarms, conformal risk budgets, shrinkage, regret-bounded policy updates, fairness/anti-starvation guards, regime detection, and rollback logic. Without an explicit composition contract, they can interfere or oscillate.

## Deliverables
- define the update order and authority boundaries among the adaptive components
- define the timescale separation between fast guards, medium-horizon policy updates, fairness-budget adjustments, and slow regime changes
- define anti-oscillation rules and hysteresis where multiple controllers interact
- document what can veto what, and in what order, when the system is under stress
- make fairness and anti-starvation guards first-class citizens in the composition matrix rather than implicit side constraints
- provide a compatibility matrix that future contributors can use before adding another controller to the ATC stack

## Acceptance Criteria
- controller ordering, veto rules, and timescale separation are explicit enough that future implementation and tests can detect oscillation, authority inversions, or fairness-controller bypasses
- the composition rules preserve calm operator behavior instead of letting adaptive components create contradictory, unfair, or rapidly flapping outputs
- future contributors can evaluate a new controller against this bead alone without re-deriving the system’s control-theory contract from chat history

## Important Notes
This bead is the missing control-theory glue. It prevents the adaptive pieces from fighting each other or quietly routing harm into whichever controller happens to be easiest to optimize.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:20:27.470687444Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:12:01.311312897Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","atc","atc-learning","atc-learning-math"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.8","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:26:13.963143140Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.8","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:20:27.470687444Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.8","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:09:55.456037892Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.8","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:26:22.889066858Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.8","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:26:32.239161146Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.8","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:26:41.448842890Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.8","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:26:50.567455727Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.8","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:26:59.654739682Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-0qt6e.3.9","title":"ATC Learning 3.9: safe policy-improvement certificates with doubly-robust off-policy evaluation and anytime-valid confidence sequences","description":"# ATC Learning 3.9: Safe policy-improvement certificates with doubly-robust off-policy evaluation and anytime-valid confidence sequences

## Why
Policy promotion is the single highest-leverage and highest-blast-radius adaptive move in the ATC design. A candidate policy can look better in simple regret tables while still being biased by selection effects, weak support overlap, optional stopping, hidden tail-risk on user-facing noise, strategically contaminated evidence, or causally confounded outcomes where multiple interventions or exogenous changes overlap. The plan needs an explicit mathematical certification layer that blocks promotion unless ATC can demonstrate, with auditable evidence, that the candidate is better enough and safe enough to replace the incumbent.

## Deliverables
- define the promotion-certificate artifact that must exist before any candidate policy revision can displace the incumbent
- define the regime-aware off-policy evaluation stack for candidate-versus-incumbent comparison using doubly-robust or augmented inverse-propensity estimation rather than raw win-rate heuristics
- define overlap/support diagnostics, contamination-evidence diagnostics, attribution-confounding diagnostics, clipped or stabilized importance-weight controls, and refusal-to-certify behavior when counterfactual support, causal isolation, or evidence trust is too weak
- define anytime-valid confidence-sequence or e-process style evidence thresholds so policy promotion remains valid under continuous monitoring and optional stopping
- define tail-risk and operator-noise protection so a candidate cannot be promoted on mean utility alone while worsening false-action spikes, spamminess, or other bad tail outcomes
- define blocker reason codes and machine-readable certificate fields for sparse data, unstable regime, overlap failure, suspicious evidence, attribution confounding, weak lower bound, tail-budget violation, or unresolved attribution backlog
- make certificates traceable to policy IDs, revisions, regimes, strata, experience slices, comparator baselines, attribution-confidence state, and any evidence-quality quarantines so later audits and surfaces can explain both promotions and non-promotions
- define what evidence can still be reused across regime boundaries and what must be reset, discounted, or explicitly marked as non-transferable

## Acceptance Criteria
- no candidate policy can be promoted without a positive, auditable improvement certificate against the incumbent rather than a heuristic score alone
- the design uses refusal-to-certify as a first-class safe outcome when overlap, attribution quality, causal isolation, regime stability, or evidence trust is inadequate
- promotion requires non-inferiority or bounded degradation on user-noise and tail-risk budgets, not just average reward improvement
- downstream summary, audit, explainability, unit-test, E2E, and rollout beads can all point to this bead as the canonical contract for why a promotion was allowed or denied

## Important Notes
This bead is the bridge between causal inference, sequential validity, tail-risk control, robustness to contaminated evidence, interference-aware attribution, and operator trust. It is deliberately stronger than a generic candidate-scoring story: the whole point is to turn policy promotion into something that is certifiable, inspectable, and safely blockable.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T22:09:13.091168149Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T01:50:18.397410255Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","atc","atc-learning","atc-learning-math"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.9","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T22:09:23.531767954Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.9","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T22:09:34.130574631Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.9","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T22:09:13.091168149Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.9","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:57:22.945388087Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.9","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:45:48.592388747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.9","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T22:09:44.522896969Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.9","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T22:09:54.959520467Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.9","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T22:11:26.468352231Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.9","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T22:11:57.113805399Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.3.9","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T22:12:35.671074419Z",  {"id":"br-0qt6e.4","title":"ATC Learning Phase 4: operator surfaces, audit artifacts, and explainability","description":"# Phase 4: operator surfaces, audit artifacts, explainability, and preflight visibility

## Why This Exists
If the adaptive ATC core becomes more sophisticated while the user-facing surfaces stay vague, stale, or post-hoc, the system will feel clever but unsafe. Phase 4 exists to make the learning system legible before, during, and after action. That includes summaries, robot and TUI/operator views, durable audit artifacts, explainability cards, bounded runtime controls, and explicit preflight impact previews for important adaptive or operator-driven changes.

## Scope
- canonical summary contracts for current learning state, freshness, fairness/burden concentration, and material change markers
- robot and TUI/operator surfaces that distinguish observation, recommendation, acted, suppressed, and deliberate no-action states
- durable archive-facing audit artifacts and machine-readable companions
- explainability cards and constrained operator controls
- preflight impact previews for policy promotions, control changes, and important pending interventions so users can see likely consequences before trusting a change

## Acceptance Criteria
- future implementation work can use this bead alone to understand why Phase 4 is not cosmetic UI work but a trust, accountability, and operator-utility layer
- Phase 4 explicitly includes before-action legibility as well as after-action auditability
- all surfaces in this phase stay aligned with the low-noise, high-actionability, and detailed-verification goals of the epic

## Important Notes
Phase 4 is where the system becomes usable to real operators and cooperating agents. If it is weak, mathematically strong internals will still feel unsafe.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:51:23.983379179Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:50:06.751492257Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","atc","atc-learning","atc-learning-phase4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:51:23.983379179Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1245,"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4","author":"codex","text":"Phase 4 is not cosmetic.  {"id":"br-0qt6e.4.1","title":"ATC Learning 4.1: strengthen ATC summary snapshots and determinism for learning state","description":"# ATC Learning 4.1: Strengthen ATC summary snapshots and determinism for learning state

## Why
The ATC summary is the main operator-facing snapshot. It needs to reflect learning state cleanly and deterministically, not just raw liveness counts.

## Deliverables
- extend the ATC summary with open experiences, resolved experiences, regret trend, calibration stress, regime state, policy revision, current execution posture, fairness or burden-concentration stress, evidence-trust/contamination suspicion state, and attribution-confidence/confoundedness state
- make observation, executed intervention, suppressed intervention, deliberate no-action, fairness/anti-starvation veto, robustness-driven distrust, and causally-confounded or \"improved but attribution uncertain\" states visible in the summary model
- surface freshness, last update source/time, degraded-data conditions, suspicious-evidence conditions, attribution-ambiguity conditions, and whether the snapshot is complete, partial, or fallback-derived
- make ordering deterministic and stable across repeated renders
- include stable change markers or delta hints so consumers can tell what materially changed since the last useful snapshot without diffing raw telemetry
- include most-impacted-target or concentration indicators so a quiet but unfair control state cannot hide behind aggregate calm
- ensure summary generation reads maintained statistics and execution-truth state instead of scanning raw rows
- document which summary fields are intended for machines vs operators and where deeper evidence/artifacts are discoverable from summary views
- keep the summary contract focused on usefulness rather than pseudo-backcompat preservation

## Acceptance Criteria
- the summary contract makes safety-gated non-action, fairness-driven throttles, contamination-driven distrust, and causally-confounded outcomes legible instead of collapsing them into unexplained silence or false certainty
- stale, degraded, partial, suspicious, attribution-confounded, or unfairly concentrated ATC state cannot masquerade as healthy calm in downstream surfaces
- machine-facing and operator-facing fields are intentionally separated rather than mixed ad hoc
- later robot, TUI, and audit surfaces can reuse this summary contract without inventing incompatible interpretations

## Important Notes
This bead is the canonical summary model for the learning system. It should make the calm/default operator experience better without hiding who is paying the price for that calm, when the evidence itself is not trustworthy, or when multiple causes mean the system cannot honestly assign credit  {"id":"br-0qt6e.4.2","title":"ATC Learning 4.2: robot ATC view for learning, adaptation, and actionability","description":"# ATC Learning 4.2: Robot ATC view for learning, adaptation, actionability, and preflight visibility

## Why
Robot mode should expose the real ATC utility surface, not a thin placeholder. Agents and scripts need a compact way to inspect the learning loop, understand whether ATC acted or deliberately did not act, and see likely consequences before trusting a pending policy change, control adjustment, or material intervention.

## Deliverables
- add robot output for open experiences, pending probes, regime state, policy trust indicators, and current execution posture
- surface recent resolved actions with their realized outcomes and delays
- explicitly distinguish observation, recommendation, executed intervention, suppressed intervention, deliberate no-op, and preview-only states
- explain why ATC acted or did not act, what the next useful action is, and what can be safely ignored
- expose risk-budget stress, rollback state, fairness or burden-concentration stress, and counterfactual adaptation context in machine-friendly form
- surface freshness, last-update provenance, and whether the view is complete, delayed, or degraded so scripts do not mistake stale state for calm healthy state
- provide stable machine-friendly fields for top-next-action, recheck guidance, change-since-last-check summaries, and preflight predicted deltas where relevant
- expose preview confidence, support weakness, stale-preview conditions, and preview-unavailable reason codes when a pending adaptive or operator-driven change should not be blindly trusted
- keep default output compact while supporting deeper views for diagnostics and artifact discovery
- ensure the robot surface remains useful in token-constrained loops and does not collapse into raw telemetry dumps

## Acceptance Criteria
- an operator or script can tell the difference between "nothing happened because nothing was wrong," "nothing happened because safety gates suppressed intervention," and "nothing has changed yet because the system is only presenting a preview"
- default output is concise and low-noise, while deeper modes expose decision IDs, experience IDs, artifact locations, preview-versus-realized links, and rationale chains
- scripts can tell whether the reported state is fresh enough to act on, whether a preview is still valid, and whether they should re-check before taking a costly step
- the robot surface answers the five user questions from 4.6 plus the preview question: what is likely to happen if this pending change is accepted or allowed to proceed

## Important Notes
This is not a vanity surface. It is the main agent-facing control panel for the learning system and must stay actionable under tight token budgets.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:57:46.117375165Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:50:07.904304679Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","atc","atc-learning","atc-learning-surfaces"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.1.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:37:35.434654378Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:05:59.058796549Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.2.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:24:01.803885328Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.2.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:25:47.556117652Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:07:05.892310204Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:07:15.252007899Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:07:24.308133647Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:07:33.551430002Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:57:46.117375165Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:06:56.491579600Z"
